---
title: "4.1.1 System software and application software"
subtitle: "System software and utilities · P150-P152"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .figure-placeholder { border: 1px dashed #8aa0bf; border-radius: 8px; background: #f4f8ff; color: #40526b; padding: 1.1em 1.3em; margin: 0.8em 0; font-size: 0.9em; text-align: center; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.74em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.34em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 4 Directory / 返回 Unit 4 目录](../../index.html)

## System Software Examples

### Compiler, linker and driver

::: {.clean-table}
| Software | Used for |
|---|---|
| <span class="term">Compiler</span> | Translates a high-level language program into machine code |
| <span class="term">Linker</span> | Combines one or more object files into a single program that can run |
| <span class="term">Device driver</span> | Enables hardware devices to communicate with the operating system |
:::

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 4.4 System software screenshot placeholder
:::

## Operating Systems

### Background software

The <span class="term">operating system</span> is software running in the background of a computer system.

Operating systems allow:

::: {.tight-list}
- input/output operations
- users to communicate with the computer
- error handling
- loading and running programs
- managing security, such as user accounts and log on passwords
:::

## Utility Programs

### Managing computer resources

<span class="term">Utility programs</span> help to manage, maintain and control computer resources.

Utility programs offered by most computer system software include:

::: {.tight-list}
- virus checkers
- defragmentation software
- disk contents analysis and repair
- file compression and file management
:::

Some utilities are initiated by the user, while virus checkers often run in the background.

## Virus Checkers

### Anti-virus software

Any computer, including mobile phones and tablets, can be subject to a virus attack.

Anti-virus software commonly:

::: {.tight-list}
- checks software or files before they are run or loaded
- compares a possible virus against a <span class="mark">database of known viruses</span>
- carries out <span class="term">heuristic checking</span> for behaviour that could indicate a possible virus
- puts possible infected files or programs into <span class="term">quarantine</span>
:::

## Virus Checkers

### Updates, scans and false positives

Anti-virus software needs to be kept up to date because new viruses are constantly discovered.

Full system checks need to be carried out regularly because some viruses lie dormant.

A file may also be a <span class="term">false positive</span>, where the user knows that the file or program is not infected by a virus.

## Defragmentation Software

### Why fragmentation slows access

As an <span class="term">HDD</span> becomes full, blocks used for files can become scattered across the disk surface.

This can happen because files are deleted, partially deleted or extended over time.

The consequence is <span class="mark">slower data access time</span>, because the HDD read-write head needs several movements to find and retrieve the data.

## Defragmentation Software

### Contiguous sectors

A disk defragmenter rearranges blocks of data to store files in <span class="term">contiguous</span> sectors wherever possible.

This gives faster data access and retrieval because the HDD needs fewer read-write head movements.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 4.5 Hard disk drive tracks and sectors screenshot placeholder
:::

## Classroom Check

### Explain utilities and system software examples

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- compilers, linkers, device drivers, operating systems and utilities are system software
- utility programs help manage, maintain and control computer resources
- anti-virus software uses known-virus databases, heuristic checking and quarantine
- anti-virus software must be kept up to date
- defragmentation rearranges data into contiguous sectors on an HDD
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Back-up, security, screensavers and device drivers

The next deck covers more utility software and device-driver details.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 4 Directory / 返回 Unit 4 目录](../../index.html)
